name: Invoicera
description:
What Invoicera Does

Invoicera is a "pure-play" invoicing tool. It’s been around for a long time and is geared towards agencies that need to track every penny, including staff expenses and billable hours. ​It’s an automated billing platform that excels at "legacy" features like purchase orders, expense attachments, and multi-layer invoice approvals.

Source: Invoicera

Key Features

🏆 Hierarchy of Approvals: Great for larger agencies where a manager needs to "approve" an invoice before it goes to the client.

​🏆 Expense Tracking: Staff can upload receipts directly to a project for reimbursement or client billing.

🏆 Time Tracking: Built-in timers that convert directly into line items on an invoice.

​Benefits of Invoicera

​It is incredibly economical. If you have hundreds of clients but don't need a "fancy" portal, Invoicera gives you professional automation for a fraction of the cost of its competitors.

​Pricing

  • ​Starter: $15/mo
  • ​Business: $39/mo
  • ​Enterprise: $79/mo
  • Infinite: $119/mo

​Pros

​✅ Very economical for high-volume invoicing.

✅ Excellent "old-school" financial features like expense management.

​Cons

​🚫 The UI feels a bit dated and "clunky" compared to modern SaaS.

🚫 Customer support can be hit-or-miss.

‍
what_people_say:
What Customers Say

​"I really liked the pricing structure, it is too economical and efficient. The dashboard feature really excites me."

– Review collected by and hosted on G2.com.
